message:
fix(swarm-scheduler): prevent autoscaling stall on zero-capacity and accept caller capacity hint

Critical fix: compute_pressure() now returns utilization = 1.0 when
there are ready/in-progress work items but zero schedulable capacity
(0 active agents * max_concurrent). Previously the system would report
0% utilization when actually starved of capacity, causing the autoscaler
to never trigger scale-up.

Also refactor evaluate_readonly() to accept max_concurrent_per_agent as
a parameter instead of hardcoding 3, letting callers pass the real
capacity hint from their configuration.

Includes rustfmt reformatting of struct variant definitions (multi-line
to single-line for simple variants like Noop, AssignWork, Rebalance,
ReclaimStale).

3 new tests:
- saturated pressure with zero capacity
- evaluate scales up with zero capacity
- evaluate_readonly uses caller capacity hint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
